steps.target: quidem ... autem (on the one hand ... but)
choices: quidem ... autem (on the one hand ... but)

hints.et ... et (both ... and): 'Et...et' expresses addition — 'quidem...autem' sets up a contrast
hints.nec ... nec (neither ... nor): 'Nec...nec' is negative — 'quidem...autem' contrasts two positive ideas

hints.genitive: Genitive singular of 'messis' is also 'messis', but here it is nominative subject
hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'messem' — 'Messis' is nominative subject

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'messis' has gen. -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'messis' has gen. -is
hints.5th declension: 5th declension has genitive in -ei — 'messis' has gen. -is

hints.accusative: Accusative neuter plural could be 'multa', but here it agrees with 'messis' (fem. nom.)
hints.ablative: Ablative feminine would be 'multa', but here it is predicate nominative with 'messis'
hints.vocative: Vocative would be for direct address — 'multa' is predicate adjective

hints.genitive: Genitive singular would be 'operarii', but context (with 'pauci') shows nom. pl.
hints.dative: Dative would be 'operariis' — 'operarii' is nominative plural subject
hints.vocative: Vocative plural would also be 'operarii', but these workers are being described, not addressed

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'operarii' ends in -i(i)
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'operarii' ends in -i(i)
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'operarii' ends in -i(i)

hints.genitive: Genitive singular could be 'pauci' for some adjective patterns, but this is nom. pl. with 'operarii'
hints.dative: Dative plural would be 'paucis' — 'pauci' is nominative plural
hints.vocative: Vocative would be for direct address — 'pauci' is predicate adjective

steps.prompt: This sentence lacks an explicit verb. What verb is implied?

hints.habet (to have): The sentence describes what the harvest 'is' and what the workers 'are' — 'esse' is implied
hints.facit (to make): No making is described — the implied verb links subject to predicate adjective
hints.venit (to come): No coming is described — the implied copula 'est/sunt' links subject and predicate

translation: The harvest indeed is great, but the workers are few
